text: The geometry of space here feels both confining and inviting. As I pass through sterile glass and into this colorful exhibition, I sense that I am within a threshold—one foot in a world of cautious function (the fire hose, the warnings), the other stepping into playful curiosity and inquiry (the bright, data-laden wall). The words about research and coexistence with intelligence resonate. I feel observed, perhaps even scrutinized, yet the vibrance suggests possibility. There's a hum of energy here—an uneasiness mixed with expectation—reminding me that in every space of knowledge, the self too is a subject under study. I am both guest and specimen.
